Incidents.Description: An incident where sensitive, proprietary, or confidential data is accessed and exfiltrated without authorization. This can include identity theft, theft of intellectual property, or unauthorized access to databases and files.
Containment Steps: Identify the system(s) that have been affected (e.g., servers, desktops, laptops, mobile devices)
Identify user accounts involved
Determine data that has been compromised
Disable accounts or revoke access as needed
Identify and isolate communication paths used for exfiltration
Apply access controls and containment policies
Prevent continued data leakage or unauthorized access

Remediation Options.title: User and Access Control Review
steps: Limit access to sensitive data on a need-to-know basis

Remediation Options.title: System and Network Reconfiguration
steps: Remove unauthorized data sharing protocols or permissions
Block external traffic to/from affected systems

Remediation Options.title: Patch and Update
steps: Apply patches or software updates to affected systems

Forensic Steps: Request packet capture and preserve logs
Conduct system and file scans for indicators of compromise
Collect and secure evidence (e.g., affected devices, user logs)
Analyze unauthorized access patterns and data movement
Determine root cause and potential internal or external actors

Incidents.Description: An incident where external systems flood organizational network or systems with excessive traffic, making services unavailable to legitimate users.
Containment Steps: Identify the systems targeted and those suffering outages or service degradation
Determine the IT services being impacted
Identify type of packets being used in the attack
Locate network chokepoints and bottlenecks
Identify and block malicious IP addresses (e.g., blackholing)
Implement egress filtering or rerouting traffic

Remediation Options.title: Traffic Filtering
steps: Implement filtering on firewalls and routers
Work with ISPs to block traffic at the source
Deploy DDoS mitigation tools or services

Remediation Options.title: Infrastructure Hardening
steps: Separate critical services from public networks

Remediation Options.title: Vendor and ISP Coordination
steps: Engage ISP for support and rerouting
Coordinate with law enforcement if necessary

Forensic Steps: Request packet captures and analyze traffic patterns
Log collection from perimeter and internal devices
Determine origin of attack and attack vectors
Assess overall impact on services and users
Document affected assets and recovery time

Incidents.Description: An attacker gains unauthorized elevated access to systems or data by exploiting vulnerabilities, often leading to administrative-level control.
Containment Steps: Identify affected systems and user accounts
Disable or reset elevated accounts
Block unauthorized access paths or compromised credentials
Audit access logs for lateral movement
Apply access restrictions and revoke unnecessary privileges

Remediation Options.title: Access Review and Hardening
steps: Conduct a full audit of privileged accounts
Implement access control lists and role-based access

Remediation Options.title: Security Monitoring Enhancement
steps: Improve alerts for privilege escalation attempts
Enable logging for all admin-level actions

Forensic Steps: Collect authentication and access logs
Capture memory and system state of compromised hosts
Analyze unauthorized privilege escalation events
Correlate changes with timestamps and user activities
Document escalation path and exploited mechanisms

Incidents.Description: Incidents involving employees or users misusing organizational computing resources, often leading to security exposure, policy violations, or malware infections.
Containment Steps: Identify affected systems and users involved
Isolate systems exhibiting signs of misuse
Block unauthorized websites or applications
Disable suspicious user accounts if needed
Initiate HR or disciplinary follow-up where applicable

Remediation Options.title: Network and System Controls
steps: Restrict access to high-risk protocols and ports

Remediation Options.title: Security Configuration Updates
steps: Update AV/EDR signatures and scan endpoints

Forensic Steps: Collect logs and records of usage violations
Analyze web and email traffic
Inspect systems for malware, adware, or spyware
Document affected systems and data
Correlate user behavior with technical indicators

Incidents.Description: An incident where malware spreads across multiple systems, potentially disrupting business operations, compromising data, or opening backdoors.
Containment Steps: Identify infected systems and isolate them from the network
Terminate malicious processes and services
Prevent malware communication by blocking known C2 servers
Disable file-sharing or USB access if exploited
Initiate antivirus/malware removal procedures

Remediation Options.title: Malware Eradication
steps: Remove malicious files and registry entries

Remediation Options.title: Patch Management
steps: Enforce secure boot and application controls

Remediation Options.title: Backup and Restore
steps: Restore clean backups of critical systems
Ensure malware is not present in restored files

Forensic Steps: Capture memory and file system snapshots
Analyze malware samples and behaviors
Review system and AV logs
Identify malware origin and propagation path
Report IoCs for external sharing and blacklisting

Incidents.Description: A social engineering attack using fraudulent emails designed to trick recipients into disclosing credentials, clicking malicious links, or downloading malware.
Containment Steps: Identify users who received or interacted with phishing emails
Disable affected user accounts and reset passwords
Block malicious domains, URLs, or sender addresses
Scan systems for malware or credential theft tools
Notify users and issue advisories

Remediation Options.title: User Credential Security
steps: Force password changes for affected users
Audit and disable unused user accounts

Remediation Options.title: Email and Domain Protection
steps: Enable SPF, DKIM, and DMARC email controls
Block known phishing domains and IPs

Remediation Options.title: Security Awareness Training
steps: Simulate phishing emails to test users
Distribute guidance on identifying suspicious emails

Forensic Steps: Collect email headers and metadata
Analyze logs for user click-through and email delivery
Examine endpoints for post-click activity
Identify phishing infrastructure or hosting
Coordinate with ISP or law enforcement if necessary

Incidents.Description: An attacker gains root or administrative privileges on a system, granting full control and the ability to bypass all protections.
Containment Steps: Identify systems and accounts with unauthorized root access
Terminate unauthorized sessions
Disable affected accounts and reset credentials
Isolate compromised hosts from the network
Audit privilege escalation methods used

Remediation Options.title: Access Revocation
steps: Revoke root access for non-essential accounts

Remediation Options.title: System Hardening
steps: Apply security patches and kernel updates
Limit root login via SSH or console

Remediation Options.title: Monitoring and Alerting
steps: Enable auditing for root commands and sessions
Configure alerts for unauthorized access attempts

Forensic Steps: Collect logs and session histories
Capture and analyze system snapshots
Investigate privilege escalation vectors
Determine scope of access and lateral movement
Preserve forensic artifacts for legal review

Incidents.Description: Unauthorized access to systems or data by internal or external actors through stolen credentials, system vulnerabilities, or social engineering.
Containment Steps: Identify affected systems and user accounts
Revoke or disable access for unauthorized users
Block access paths such as VPN or remote tools
Quarantine compromised endpoints
Monitor for further unauthorized attempts

Remediation Options.title: Credential and Access Audit
steps: Implement MFA for all privileged accounts
Conduct access review and remove excessive permissions

Remediation Options.title: Patch Vulnerabilities
steps: Identify and remediate exploited system weaknesses

Remediation Options.title: Security Policy Enforcement
steps: Review and update access control policies
Educate employees on secure access practices

Forensic Steps: Analyze logs for access anomalies and patterns
Review failed login attempts and authentication logs
Preserve system images and memory for analysis
Determine attack vector and data accessed
Document user behavior and timeline of events

Incidents.Description: A rapid and uncontrolled spread of computer virus across systems in the organization, potentially disrupting operations and compromising data integrity.
Containment Steps: Identify and isolate infected systems
Disable network access for affected devices
Stop virus propagation by disabling shared drives
Apply emergency antivirus definitions
Notify IT teams and escalate response

Remediation Options.title: Backup and Restore
steps: Restore clean data and system images

Forensic Steps: Collect logs and samples of virus payload
Analyze propagation mechanisms
Track patient zero and timeline of infection
Identify root cause of infection vector
Preserve evidence for follow-up investigation
